county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1862 as original Dakota Territory county. Named for Abraham Lincoln. Canton is near Sioux Falls; Lincoln County has become part of the Sioux Falls metropolitan area.
county_metadata.research_tips: Lincoln County Register of Deeds in Canton holds land records from 1862 — among SD's oldest. As an original Dakota Territory county, early records may also be at SDSHS. Canton is the site of the Hiawatha Indian Insane Asylum (1902-1934), now a national historic landmark; records at NARA document Native American patients from across the country. BLM GLO homestead records cover original entries. Lincoln County Clerk of Courts has probate, naturalization, and civil records. Norwegian and Scandinavian settlers predominated. The Canton Sioux Valley News newspaper is on microfilm at SDSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The county's proximity to Sioux Falls means city directory and Sioux Falls newspapers supplement county records. Canton's Augustana Academy (Lutheran, 1884-1971) records are at Augustana University archives.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Grantor/grantee deed books from 1862 to present. Register of Deeds maintains index books and recorded instruments.
Marriage licenses and certificates from 1862. Register of Deeds issued licenses; state registration began 1905.

source_profiles.source_name: Lincoln County Birth and Death Records
source_profiles.repository: South Dakota Vital Records, Pierre / Register of Deeds
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State registration from 1905; county-filed records may predate statewide compliance. Certified copies from SD Department of Health Vital Records. Some counties have earlier records filed with the Register of Deeds.

source_profiles.source_name: Lincoln County Clerk of Courts Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate, guardianship, adoption, naturalization, civil and criminal case files from 1862. Clerk of Courts maintains court records for the circuit court.

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Lincoln County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Lincoln County including homestead, preemption, timber culture, and cash entries. South Dakota was entirely public domain — GLO records are the primary source for first land ownership.
Federal population schedules for Lincoln County. Dakota Territory enumerated from 1860. Agricultural schedules (1850-1880) provide additional family and economic data.

source_profiles.repository: South Dakota State Historical Society / Chronicling America
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historical newspapers from Lincoln County communities. SDSHS holds the largest collection of SD newspapers on microfilm. Chronicling America has digitized SD papers.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1893 from unorganized territory. Named for W.P. Lyman, Dakota Territory official. Contains portions of Lower Brule Indian Reservation and Crow Creek Reservation across the Missouri.
county_metadata.research_tips: Lyman County Register of Deeds in Kennebec holds deed records from 1893. Lower Brule Indian Reservation borders the county across the Missouri River — BIA records at NARA Kansas City include census rolls, allotment records, and agency files for Lower Brule Sioux families. Fort Hale (1880-1884) records at NARA document military presence. BLM GLO homestead records cover original land entries. Lyman County Clerk of Courts has probate and civil records. Big Bend Dam (1963) and Lake Sharpe flooded Missouri River bottomlands; pre-dam land records are important. The Kennebec and Presho newspapers at SDSHS cover community history. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. Ranching and farming are the primary economies; livestock brand records identify ranching families.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Grantor/grantee deed books from 1893 to present. Register of Deeds maintains index books and recorded instruments.
Marriage licenses and certificates from 1893. Register of Deeds issued licenses; state registration began 1905.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1873 from Hanson County. Named for Edwin S. McCook, Dakota Territory secretary. Agricultural county in southeastern SD.
county_metadata.research_tips: McCook County Register of Deeds in Salem holds deed records from 1873. BLM GLO homestead records document original land entries. German-Russian, Norwegian, and Swedish settlers predominated. McCook County Clerk of Courts in Salem has probate, naturalization, and civil records. The Salem Special newspaper is on microfilm at SDSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. Mennonite and Hutterite communities are present in the county — Mennonite church records at the Center for Mennonite Brethren Studies and Hutterite colony records are important sources. Lutheran and Catholic church records serve the Scandinavian and German communities. The county's agricultural economy centered on wheat, corn, and livestock.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1873. Named for Gen. James B. McPherson. Located along the North Dakota border in north-central SD.
county_metadata.research_tips: McPherson County Register of Deeds in Leola holds deed records from 1873. German-Russian (Black Sea Germans) settlers from the Odessa region were predominant — the Germans from Russia Heritage Society (GRHS) in Bismarck, ND has extensive family files, immigration records, and village histories. German Evangelical, Baptist, and Catholic church records are essential sources. BLM GLO homestead records document original claims. McPherson County Clerk of Courts in Leola has probate, naturalization, and civil records. The Leola McPherson County Herald newspaper is on microfilm at SDSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. Located on the ND border — check North Dakota county records for related families. The Eureka community (across the border in ND) was a major German-Russian center with overlapping family connections.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Grantor/grantee deed books from 1873 to present. Register of Deeds maintains index books and recorded instruments.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1885 from Day County. Named for Marshall Vincent, Dakota Territory official. Lake region county in northeastern SD.
county_metadata.research_tips: Marshall County Register of Deeds in Britton holds deed records from 1885. Sisseton-Wahpeton Oyate (Lake Traverse Reservation) extends into Marshall County — BIA records at NARA Kansas City include census rolls, allotment records, and school records for Sisseton-Wahpeton Dakota families. BLM GLO homestead records document settlers who filed claims. Marshall County Clerk of Courts in Britton has probate, naturalization, and civil records. Scandinavian and German settlers predominated. The Britton Journal newspaper is on microfilm at SDSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. Day County holds some pre-1885 records. The county's lake region attracted both farmers and resort-seekers.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Grantor/grantee deed books from 1885 to present. Register of Deeds maintains index books and recorded instruments.
Marriage licenses and certificates from 1885. Register of Deeds issued licenses; state registration began 1905.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1889 from Lawrence County. Named for Gen. George Meade (Gettysburg). Sturgis is known for the annual motorcycle rally. Contains Fort Meade (1878-1944).
county_metadata.research_tips: Meade County Register of Deeds in Sturgis holds deed records from 1889. Fort Meade (1878-1944) was a major military post — records at NARA include muster rolls, post returns, and hospital records documenting soldiers and families. The Old Fort Meade Museum has local military history collections. BLM GLO homestead records document settlers. Meade County Clerk of Courts has probate, civil, and naturalization records. The Black Hills area attracted miners, ranchers, and homesteaders. The Sturgis Tribune newspaper is on microfilm at SDSHS. Lawrence County holds pre-1889 records. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The Bear Butte area has deep Lakota and Cheyenne sacred site significance. The Sturgis Motorcycle Rally (1938+) has generated economic records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Grantor/grantee deed books from 1889 to present. Register of Deeds maintains index books and recorded instruments.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1909. Named for Arthur C. Mellette, first SD governor. Contains portions of Rosebud Reservation. Organized when reservation lands were opened.
county_metadata.research_tips: Mellette County Register of Deeds in White River holds records from 1909. Rosebud Indian Reservation extends into Mellette County — BIA records at NARA Kansas City include census rolls, allotment records, and agency correspondence for Sicangu Lakota families. BLM GLO homestead records document settlers who filed on opened reservation lands. Mellette County Clerk of Courts has probate and civil records. The White River Herald newspaper is on microfilm at SDSHS. Ranching is the primary economy; livestock brand records identify families. Due to reservation overlap, researchers must consult both county and tribal/BIA records. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed county records. Rosebud Agency records document tribal enrollment and land allotments.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Grantor/grantee deed books from 1909 to present. Register of Deeds maintains index books and recorded instruments.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1873 from Hanson County. Named for Nelson Miner, early territorial legislator. Agricultural county in east-central SD.
county_metadata.research_tips: Miner County Register of Deeds in Howard holds deed records from 1873. BLM GLO homestead records document original land entries. Scandinavian (Norwegian) and German settlers predominated. Miner County Clerk of Courts in Howard has probate, naturalization, and civil records. The Howard Miner County Pioneer newspaper is on microfilm at SDSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. Lutheran church records are especially important for the Norwegian community. The county's agricultural economy centered on grain and livestock. The Great Dakota Boom (1878-1887) brought rapid settlement. Township histories and county history books document pioneer families.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1862 as original Dakota Territory county. Sioux Falls is SD's largest city and commercial center. Named for Minnehaha Falls (Minnehaha Creek). County is the most populated in SD.
county_metadata.research_tips: Minnehaha County Register of Deeds in Sioux Falls holds land records from 1862 — among SD's oldest. Sioux Falls is SD's largest city; the Siouxland Heritage Museums (Old Courthouse Museum and Pettigrew Home) hold extensive local and regional genealogy collections. BLM GLO homestead records cover original entries. Minnehaha County Clerk of Courts has extensive probate, naturalization, and civil records due to the large urban population. The Sioux Falls Argus Leader newspaper (1881+) is SD's newspaper of record, on microfilm at SDSHS and Sioux Falls Public Library. City directories for Sioux Falls (1880s+) document residents and occupations. Augustana University archives in Sioux Falls hold Scandinavian community records and Lutheran church archives. Sioux Falls was a divorce destination (short residency requirement) — divorce records are at the Clerk of Courts. The SD State Penitentiary (1881+) in Sioux Falls has inmate records at SDSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Grantor/grantee deed books from 1862 to present. Register of Deeds maintains index books and recorded instruments.
Marriage licenses and certificates from 1862. Register of Deeds issued licenses; state registration began 1905.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1873 from Brookings and Minnehaha counties. Named for Gideon Moody, Dakota Territory legislator and first US Senator from SD. Home to the Flandreau Santee Sioux Reservation.
county_metadata.research_tips: Moody County Register of Deeds in Flandreau holds deed records from 1873. The Flandreau Santee Sioux Reservation is within the county — BIA records at NARA Kansas City include census rolls, allotment records, and school records (Flandreau Indian School, est. 1893) for Santee Dakota families. BLM GLO homestead records document non-Indian settlers. Moody County Clerk of Courts has probate, naturalization, and civil records. The Flandreau Indian School was a major off-reservation boarding school — student records at NARA cover Native families from across the Great Plains. The Moody County Enterprise newspaper is on microfilm at SDSHS. Scandinavian settlers were prominent. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The Flandreau area was settled early by both Santee Dakota people and Euro-American homesteaders.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Grantor/grantee deed books from 1873 to present. Register of Deeds maintains index books and recorded instruments.
